summary: Read-only systems review completed. The diff stays within allowed paths, keeps sim-core free of UI/Electron/Node IO dependencies, avoids M4 campaign/battle/siege/supply scope, and all requested verification commands passed. Requesting changes because the apply command can produce inconsistent authority/obligation state across repeated postwar arrangements, and restore-vassal-ruler can be accepted without actually restoring or retaining a ruler.

tests_run.result: On feat/m3-postwar-governance. Modified/untracked files are task-scoped; review handoff was added after inspection.
Task JSON parsed; required keys present; status REVIEW and route_to systems_architect.

tests_run.result: No new Math.random/Date.now/performance.now or UI/Electron/Node IO dependency in reviewed files. M4 scope terms only appeared in task/handoff text or negative protocol test data.
14 files passed; 91 tests passed. Existing Node MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning observed.
Node/worker hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; root Vitest 24 files/151 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.
Workspace typecheck passed for packages with typecheck scripts.
ESLint completed with no reported errors.

A restore-vassal-ruler settlement followed by a tribute-only settlement for the same victor/local/district but different settlementId was accepted and left 3 active obligations with validateWorldStateV0 reporting 0 invariant errors.
A direct-control settlement after a restored-vassal settlement was accepted; district controller became victor while prior local-vassal obligations and suzerain edge remained.
restore-vassal-ruler with localRulerCharacterId null and policyId null was accepted, created 2 obligations, and created 0 enfeoffments.

decisions.reason: The command path can accept sequences that violate the task's authority/obligation invariants even though current tests pass.

decisions.decision: No allowed_paths or forbidden_paths violation found.
decisions.evidence: Changed/untracked files are under packages/sim-core, packages/protocol, tests, project/tasks/thread-registry.json, active task JSON, and project/messages.

decisions.decision: No packages/save-format change found; existing M3 save rejection remains acceptable for this task.

risks.issue: Duplicate or conflicting postwar arrangements can be applied to the same district when settlementId changes. The duplicate guard only checks the same postwar source prefix for non-direct methods, while new obligations are always appended and old obligations/suzerain state are not retired. This allows overlapping tax/levy obligations and direct control with retained vassal obligations.

restore-vassal-ruler does not require a localRulerCharacterId or policyId. If either is null, the command is still accepted, obligations are created, and the event reports restore-vassal-ruler without a restored/retained ruler enfeoffment.

Negative coverage only rejects duplicate settlementId and suzerain cycles. It does not cover repeated same-district arrangements with a different settlementId, direct-after-vassal cleanup/rejection, or restore-vassal-ruler with missing ruler/policy.

blockers: Fix the apply command so a district cannot accumulate conflicting postwar settlement rights/obligations. Minimal acceptable repair: reject an already-arranged victor/local/district unless a future explicit replacement command is designed with retirement events and tests.
Validate restore-vassal-ruler payload consistency. Require a valid local ruler and policy, or rename/split the method if null means a different governance mode.
Add regression tests for different-settlementId duplicate arrangements, direct-after-vassal or vassal-after-direct behavior, and restore-vassal-ruler null ruler/policy rejection.
requested_action: Route back to original writer thread 019effbf-bfc9-7f30-8e2d-7c290d1080a3 for targeted repair, then return to systems_architect review.
